notes: Health after create moved from p1_total 43 / enabled 42 to p1_total 44 / enabled 43.
Rollback restored p1_total 43 / enabled 42 and original config hashes.
The audit helper's create_rule_present and import_merge_present flags remained false, but live health counters prove the write path mutated and restored the config.

findings.title: Representative P1 rule detection matches manual review on Benchmark and GoldenTime.
findings.details: Required manual rules were all present: Benchmark matched PERF-SETMULTIVALUE-ADOPT-01, ACTIVE-01, EXC-TRY-01, EXC-DP-01; GoldenTime matched HARD-01, STYLE-IDX-01, PERF-EV-01, HARD-03.

findings.title: txt/pnl canonical boundary handling is now consistent.
findings.details: Both txt and pnl alias paths resolved to the same 38-row canonical finding set, with UNKNOWN rows = 0 and matching UI/API/Excel/summary counts.

findings.title: Autofix prepare/apply contract is still inconsistent for some proposals.
findings.details: Copy-file prepare attempts reported allow_apply=true for several issues, but apply later returned HTTP 409 semantic-guard blocks for EXC-DP-01, PERF-SETMULTIVALUE-ADOPT-01, and EXC-TRY-01. The product safely blocks risky changes, but the prepare contract still overstates apply readiness.

findings.title: Live AI compare/prepare is usable, but compare-modal apply remains conservatively blocked.
findings.details: Live AI generate, compare, and prepare completed successfully and produced a placeholder-free diff, but the compare modal kept apply disabled with proposal_missing. This is safe behavior, but the UI contract is still not fully self-explanatory.

findings.title: Rules write/rollback round-trip restores correctly.
findings.details: Create, update, delete, import dry-run, and rollback altered rule health as expected and restored the original config hashes at the end of the audit.

current_unimplemented_or_misbehaving_are: Autofix prepare can still advertise allow_apply=true for proposals later rejected by semantic guard during apply.
Live compare modal can show a prepared diff while the apply button stays blocked with proposal_missing, which is safe but confusing.
top_10_improvements: Make /api/autofix/prepare run the same semantic guard path used by apply so allow_apply cannot be optimistic.
Expose candidate-specific blocked reasons in the compare modal instead of a generic proposal_missing message.
Return prepared proposal ids directly from compare/prepare so the UI can explain why a candidate is or is not applyable.
Add a dedicated regression test for the prepare=true but apply=409 mismatch.
Add a stable rules-write revision id or echo payload so live create/import verification does not rely on heuristic presence scans.
Expose generated Excel row counts in API payloads to make UI/report parity checks easier to audit.
Add a release-gate assertion for Benchmark and GoldenTime representative manual-rule sets.
Surface detected encoding and canonical source in more visible UI locations for txt/pnl files.
Persist triage and compare modal telemetry in a structured audit artifact to simplify future full audits.
Keep rotating audit artifacts automatically so future full audits do not have to sift through stale session output.

final_verdict.summary: The current product passes representative WinCC OA rule-detection checks and maintains UI/API/Excel parity, with the main remaining gap in autofix prepare/apply contract clarity rather than rule detection itself.
